# Project Context: self-hosted-radio

<!-- Auto-generated by /cortex-spec from spec artifacts. -->
<!-- All agents reference this file for project-level conventions. -->

## Tech Stack

- **Icecast2** (`moul/icecast`) — HTTP audio streaming server; stream at `/stream.mp3`, status at `/status-json.xsl`
- **Liquidsoap** (`savonet/liquidsoap:2.2`) — MP3 encoder and playout engine; reads M3U playlist, pushes via TCP to Icecast
- **Caddy v2** — existing reverse proxy; owns 80/443, automatic Let's Encrypt, proxies `radio.calenwalshe.com`
- **Flask / `src/web/app.py`** — existing web app extended with Windy API cam proxy
- **Docker Compose** — all services on external `agent-stack` network; no new networks introduced
- **Python 3.11+** — dj_mixer.py, web app, cam proxy
- **httpx** — HTTP client for Windy API calls (already installed)

## Conventions

- Icecast and Liquidsoap configs live in `agent-stack/icecast/` and `agent-stack/liquidsoap/`
- Caddy config is `agent-stack/caddy/Caddyfile` — validate before every reload
- Python source in `src/`, static web assets in `src/web/static/`
- Smart queue playlist written to `/queue/playlist.m3u` (Docker volume shared between dj_mixer and Liquidsoap)
- Windy API key stored in `config/webcams.json` — never exposed to browser; proxy endpoint only
- Camera database in `config/cameras.json` — used by cam proxy for cam selection
- Track metadata in `config/track_metadata.json` — pre-analyzed offline by essentia

## Architecture Rules

- Icecast runs on internal port 8100 only — never exposed to host directly; accessed via Caddy reverse proxy
- `<public>0</public>` is mandatory in every Icecast mount config — stream must not appear in public directories
- Liquidsoap connects to Icecast via Docker service name (`host="icecast"`) on port 8100
- No PulseAudio or ALSA needed — Liquidsoap reads files and pushes encoded audio over TCP
- Windy API calls go server-side only — `/cam-url/<cam_id>` endpoint in `web/app.py`; browser never touches the API directly
- Dynamic playlist file is the only control interface between dj_mixer and Liquidsoap in v1
- All new Docker services join the `agent-stack` external network; no new networks
- Caddy is the only service with ports 80/443; everything else is internal

## Write Boundaries

- `src/` — Python source and web assets
- `config/` — camera database, stream config (read-mostly)
- `agent-stack/icecast/` — Icecast config files (new)
- `agent-stack/liquidsoap/` — Liquidsoap scripts (new)
- `agent-stack/docker-compose.yml` — add new services only; do not modify existing service definitions
- `agent-stack/caddy/Caddyfile` — add new virtual host block; do not modify existing blocks
